name: no_node_id_empty_range
reason: An empty range `[idx, idx)` vacuously satisfies `LogRangeNoNodeId` — tests that the base case holds regardless of log contents

name: concat_no_node_extends_match
code:
proof fn phi_2_concat_no_node_extends_match<DT: Dispatch>(
    queue: Seq<ReqId>,
    log: Map<LogIdx, LogEntry<DT>>,
    nodeId: NodeId,
    updates: Map<ReqId, UpdateState<DT>>,
    a: LogIdx,
    b: LogIdx,
    c: LogIdx,
)
    requires
        a <= b <= c,
        LogRangeNoNodeId(log, a, b, nodeId),
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, 0, b, c, nodeId, updates),
    ensures
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, 0, a, c, nodeId, updates),
{
    concat_LogRangeNoNodeId_LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, 0, a, b, c, nodeId, updates);
}
reason: Prepending a no-node-id range to a matching range extends the match — tests that remote-only log entries can be freely prepended

name: all_remote_empty_queue
reason: A log range with no local entries matches an empty queue — tests that all-remote ranges require zero queue elements

name: no_node_id_single_entry
code:
proof fn phi_4_no_node_id_single_entry<DT: Dispatch>(
    log: Map<LogIdx, LogEntry<DT>>,
    idx: LogIdx,
    nodeId: NodeId,
    remoteId: NodeId,
)
    requires
        log.contains_key(idx),
        log[idx].node_id == remoteId,
        remoteId != nodeId,
    ensures
        LogRangeNoNodeId::<DT>(log, idx, idx + 1, nodeId),
{
}
reason: A single remote entry satisfies LogRangeNoNodeId — tests the one-element inductive case

name: no_node_id_ignores_extra_keys
